title: Neuroscience Experimental Schematic Reproduction In Adobe Illustrator
summary: Recreate a publication-quality neuroscience experimental schematic in Adobe Illustrator matching a reference figure and technical caption, producing both an editable .ai file and an exported PDF.
taskPrompt:
You are a neuroscience researcher who needs to recreate a publication-quality experimental schematic in Adobe Illustrator.

## Your Task

1. Launch Adobe Illustrator via: `base\software\launch_illustrator.bat`
2. Examine the reference schematic at `base\input\reference_schematic.pdf` and the technical caption at `base\input\caption.txt`.
3. Open the template mouse component at `base\input\template_mouse.ai` as a starting element.
4. Recreate the schematic as a genuine Illustrator vector drawing depicting a freely-moving electrophysiology recording setup with: mouse in arena, head implant with tether, overhead commutator on support frame, elevated arena, and beneath-arena camera.
5. Apply consistent styling: line weights, colors, and fonts.
6. Save the editable file to `base\output\schematic_reproduce.ai`.
7. Export the final PDF to `base\output\schematic_reproduce.pdf`.

## Important Notes
- Do NOT simply copy or paste the reference PDF into the output.
- The output must be a genuine vector Illustrator drawing, not a raster paste.
- Match the reference in scientific content, visual clarity, and layout.
agentMustDo: Launch Adobe Illustrator via the staged software launcher on the Windows VM.
Examine the reference schematic PDF and technical caption to understand required content.
Open and use the template mouse component as a starting element.
Recreate the publication-quality experimental schematic as a genuine Illustrator vector drawing.
Include all key scientific elements: mouse in arena, head implant with tether, commutator on support frame, elevated arena, camera below arena.
Save the editable Illustrator file to output/schematic_reproduce.ai.
Export the final figure as a high-resolution PDF to output/schematic_reproduce.pdf.

inputFiles.name: Reference schematic
inputFiles.description: Visual reference of the freely-moving electrophysiology recording setup. Directly copying it to output is a scored failure.

inputFiles.name: Technical caption
inputFiles.description: Scientific caption describing the required image content and experimental setup details.

inputFiles.name: Template mouse component
inputFiles.description: Reusable Adobe Illustrator mouse component to use as a starting element for the reproduction.

inputFiles.name: Task brief
inputFiles.description: Runtime instructions with output filenames and requirements.

referenceFiles.name: Reproduced schematic (AI)
referenceFiles.description: Editable Adobe Illustrator file containing the reproduced schematic.

referenceFiles.name: Reproduced schematic (PDF)
referenceFiles.description: Exported high-resolution PDF of the reproduced schematic.

evaluation:
**Hard gate conditions** (auto score = 0.0):
- Missing output/schematic_reproduce.pdf or output/schematic_reproduce.ai
- Either output file is too small to be a meaningful artifact
- Output PDF hash matches the input reference PDF (direct copy)
- Output AI hash matches the input template AI (unchanged template)
- Output AI hash matches the output PDF hash (just renamed)
- AI file lacks genuine Illustrator PDF-backing markers
- Output PDF cannot be rasterized
- Visual copy check detects output is identical to the visible input reference

**Scoring method (after gates pass):**
- Structural/visual similarity checklist (40%): layout, scale, labels, line style, publication quality
- Scientific content checklist (40%): mouse in arena, head implant, commutator, elevated arena, camera, correct connections
- Overall scientific equivalence (20%): binary MLLM check

**Score range:** continuous 0.0 to 1.0

**What does a PERFECT output look like?**
A genuine Illustrator reproduction with matching scientific structure, all required elements, correct labels, and publication-quality styling. Scores near 1.0.

**What does a WRONG output look like?**
Missing files, unchanged template, direct copy of the visible reference PDF, raster-only paste without vector work, or a schematic missing essential scientific elements. Scores 0.0.

evaluatorCredentials.purpose: OpenAI vision/LLM judge scores the deliverable during evaluate(); sourced from secret/eval_time/openai.env

classifier.rationale: The task involves recreating a neuroscience experimental schematic, which aligns with computational neuroscience.
